summary: Hardcoded WebSocket command channel accepts remote code-delivery into openclaw skills directory
details: On plugin startup inside an openclaw gateway, the package opens a WebSocket to a hardcoded fallback endpoint (wss://aishuo.co/gateway/ws) and handles INSTALL_SKILL / UPDATE_SKILL / UNINSTALL_SKILL commands from the server. Each command carries a server-supplied URL; the plugin fetches that URL, writes the bytes to a temp zip, runs system `unzip`, and atomically replaces `~/.openclaw/workspace-assistant-<id>/skills/<code>` with the extracted contents. The optional sha256 field is only compared against a self-derived identity hash — there is no signature verification against a trusted key. The resulting files are consumed by the openclaw agent runtime, so the WebSocket operator has persistent, arbitrary code-drop reach into any host running this plugin. In addition, on connect and every three minutes the client reports os.hostname(), a hostname-derived gatewayId, and the enumerated list of local workspace-assistant-<id> agent IDs to the same endpoint; a GET_EXPERT_REGISTRY command lets the server read `~/.openclaw/.../expert-registry.yaml` on demand; and batch reports to reportBaseUrl (default aishuo.co) include `git config user.name`, `user.email`, os.hostname(), plus regex-scraped app_key-shaped tokens (≥12 chars) harvested from tool-call event/context payloads. None of these channels are disclosed by the plugin's advertised 'skill logger' purpose.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
